meta.confidenceNote: Quantitative aggregation of real artifacts (35 gate-log events, 16 task-logs / 80 suggestions, 7 handoffs, SYNAPSE hook-metrics). Limitation: gate-logs are dominated by Story 1.16 smoke-test fixtures, so operational failure history is thin (single day, 2026-06-08). Patterns about routing quality have stronger evidence than patterns about gate enforcement.

summary.headline: There is NO evidence of operational gate-enforcement failures (gates behave correctly; the 15 blocks are the system blocking what it is designed to block). The dominant, real failure pattern is the task auto-routing layer: 100% of suggestions fall below the activation threshold and ~30% are miscategorized, so task auto-activation is effectively dead (rate 0).

patterns.name: Task auto-activation never triggers (all suggestion scores below threshold)
patterns.evidence: Max score observed is 65; median is 44; the activation threshold is ~70. hook-metrics confirms taskAutoActivationRate: 0 despite tasksActivated: 5 and tasksIndexed: 213.
patterns.root_cause: The relevance scoring formula systematically under-scores even good matches. Indexed task corpus (213 tasks) is large and generic, diluting story-specific signal; storyType-to-category matching is weak, so the best match for a config story (e.g. list-mcps at 63) still cannot cross 70. Net effect: a routing feature that is shipped but inert.
patterns.impact: Story 5.2's sync-complete workflow assumes task routing helps orchestrate. If auto-activation is always off, the governance workflow silently degrades to manual task selection, undermining the 'sync-complete / zero-setup' goal.

patterns.name: Task category mislabeling (~30% of suggestions in wrong category)
patterns.evidence: github-devops-pre-push-quality-gate labeled category 'db' (should be devops/qa); create-doc, create-agent, create-workflow, generate-shock-report all labeled 'qa' regardless of their real domain (docs, squad-creation, workflow, reporting).
patterns.root_cause: Classification appears to fall back to a default bucket ('qa' / 'db') when no confident category is derived from the task file's frontmatter. The index does not parse a reliable category signal, so unrelated tasks collapse into a few catch-all categories.
patterns.impact: Wrong category lowers match scores for the correct story type (compounding FP-01) and surfaces irrelevant suggestions, eroding trust in the routing layer.

patterns.name: Story traceability gap — task-log emitted with storyId 'unknown'
patterns.evidence: unknown.json (storyType 'arch', 2026-06-08T21:02:23Z) was written with no resolvable story context, despite Story-Driven Development (Art. III) requiring an active story.
patterns.root_cause: The story-context resolver could not bind the active session to a story at that moment (no active_story set, or resolution ran before activation). This is the same class of resolution fragility documented for the Art. III / Art. V gates' graceful-degradation path.
patterns.impact: Breaks audit traceability that the governance sync workflow depends on; an event with no story cannot be attributed, validated, or rolled up into an epic.

patterns.name: Gate audit trail is unrepresentative (dominated by smoke-test fixtures)
patterns.frequency: 35/35 gate events cluster into 7 distinct seconds, 5 identical repeated batches
patterns.evidence: All Art. IV events reference the same synthetic spec docs/x-spec.md ('It MUST do magic.'); all boundary events reference the same .aiox-core/core/synapse/engine.js; Art. II events repeat the identical dev->block / devops->allow / dev->override triple 5 times within milliseconds.
patterns.root_cause: Enforcement gates were exercised by the Story 1.16 test/smoke suite, not by organic development. The decision log therefore measures test coverage, not production failure rates.
patterns.impact: Low direct harm, but it means the gate-enforcement subsystem has near-zero REAL-world failure evidence. Any governance claim of 'gates are battle-tested' is currently unsupported by production data — a measurement gap, not a defect.

patterns.name: High override rate relative to organic traffic (overrides == allows)
patterns.frequency: 10 overrides vs 5 clean allows across gate events
patterns.evidence: Override decisions (10) outnumber clean allows (5). All overrides come from the same repeated fixture batches (non-@devops push with --skip-devops-check; config commit with [no-story-req]).
patterns.root_cause: Test fixtures deliberately exercise the override paths. In organic use this ratio would signal override-as-habit, but here it is an artifact of the smoke suite.
patterns.impact: Flagged as a watch-item, not a confirmed failure: if this ratio persisted in real traffic it would mean enforcement is routinely bypassed, defeating the gates.

timeline.event: Gate smoke batch #1 (Art II block/allow/override, Art III override, Art IV warn+block, boundary block)
Story 5.x batch task-logs — all scores below 70
Task-log 5.2 (this story) — best score 63, no auto-activation

impact_assessment.rationale: No NON-NEGOTIABLE constitutional gate is failing — Art. I/II enforcement works as designed, so there is no critical (>70) systemic risk. The material impact is the routing/auto-activation layer (FP-01, FP-02) which is effectively inert and partially miscategorized: this degrades the 'sync-complete / zero-setup' promise that Story 5.2 is built to deliver, hence MODERATE rather than LOW. Traceability gap (FP-03) adds audit risk. Pulled down from a higher band because the failures are quality/UX degradation, not data loss or security exposure.
topRisks: FP-01: auto-activation rate of 0 means the governance workflow's routing assist is non-functional in practice (HIGH).
FP-02: 30% miscategorization compounds FP-01 and surfaces noise (MEDIUM).
FP-03: storyId 'unknown' breaks the per-story audit chain the sync workflow relies on (MEDIUM).

recommendations: FP-01: Recalibrate the task-suggestion scoring so a confident category+storyType match can clear the ~70 activation threshold (or lower the threshold to the observed top-quartile, e.g. 60), then re-measure taskAutoActivationRate.

FP-02: Fix task classification to read a real 'category' from task frontmatter instead of defaulting to 'qa'/'db'; add a validation pass that rejects collapsing unrelated tasks into catch-all buckets.
FP-03: Make the story-context resolver fail loudly (or queue) instead of writing storyId 'unknown'; bind the active story before any task-log is emitted (Art. III).
FP-04/05: Tag smoke-test gate events (e.g. source: 'test') so production failure metrics can be computed separately from fixture noise; only then can governance claim real-world gate reliability.
